creator: alepulver@FreeBSD.org
text:
When compiling the lang/clisp port on a FreeBSD 4.x chroot (Tinderbox "jail"), I got the following make(1) messages a few times:

make in free(): warning: junk pointer, too high to make sense
make in free(): warning: page is already free

In pointyhat, instead, it just dumps a core file:

make in free(): error: junk pointer, too high to make sense
Abort trap (core dumped)
*** Error code 134

The pointyhat log is here:
http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.4.2006071303/clisp-2.38_1.log

How-To-Repeat: 

Try to build lang/clisp in FreeBSD 4.x.
